title: Pair 03: Coarse vs Fine Queue Buckets
summary: This comparison keeps the dim sum hall, a large-party-heavy demand wave, and booking rules fixed, but changes how detailed the party-size buckets become. Option A uses broader bands that are easier for hosts to scan, while Option B uses finer buckets to see whether better seat matching is worth the extra operational attention.

A.label: Broader queue bands
A.notes: The banquet floor is easy to manage when hosts only watch three bands, but medium and large parties compete inside the same queue.

B.label: Fine-grained queue bands
B.notes: Hosts separate one-top, two-top, and family-size demand more tightly so large round tables are not held up by mixed queues.
